scores.correctness_notes: Covers the change/task model and waiting patterns reasonably well, but misses key details from the gold standard: the CLI commands (pebble changes, pebble tasks), the REST API endpoints (GET /v1/changes/{id}, /v1/changes/{id}/wait), the state persistence to $PEBBLE/.pebble.state, and the notices/change-update mechanism. Focuses heavily on the ops/Python client perspective while the gold standard covers all interaction methods.
scores.hallucination_notes: container.pebble.get_change() and container.pebble.wait_change() — the gold standard doesn't mention these as direct container.pebble methods; change.err as an attribute is not in the gold standard; container.replan() returning a ChangeID is questionable (the gold standard says ops methods automatically wait). The response implies lower-level client usage that may not match actual ops API signatures.
